preamble: The shift from [ʌ] to the open-mid front vowel [ε] is a key feature of several modern English chain shifts. As front vowels like [ε] and [æ] lower or back, the STRUT vowel [ʌ] is pulled forward into the vacated space.

examples.note: STRUT fronting toward [ε] is noted by Eckert but lacks robust quantitative attestation in the CVS literature.
STRUT vowel fronts in these dialects; NZ realization tends toward [ɵ] or [ɜ] rather than [ɛ] precisely.
